text:
Nitrate pollution in water resources has caused serious harm to the environment and human health, so it is urgent to develop highly efficient removal technologies. While single-atom alloys (SAAs) were demonstrated as high-performance bimetallic catalysts in various fields, here we discovered a drastic contrast between the performances of SAA catalysts in the thermocatalytic nitrate reduction reaction (T-NRR) and the electrocatalytic nitrate reduction reaction (E-NRR).

We synthesized and evaluated a range of Pd/Cu nanoalloys across the Pd:Cu composition range from 100:1 to 1:100 and observed opposite trends of catalytic performance for the two systems. Specifically, the Pd/Cu(1:100) SAA exhibited the highest catalytic performance in the electrocatalytic route, with the highest value of TOFₚ₀ = 2 min⁻¹ and a very high selectivity toward N₂ of 94%. Conversely, the SAA had the lowest performance compared to its corresponding nanoalloys in the thermocatalytic route.

The cause of the different catalytic activities of Pd/Cu (1:100) SAA toward E-NRR was further investigated by using density functional theory (DFT). Compared with other catalysts, the Pd/Cu (1:100) SAA showed higher activity toward E-NRR. It was found that the NO₃* species on the Pd/Cu (1:100) SAA was much more stable in the electrochemical system. Meanwhile, because of the localized pH environment in the electrochemical system and the capacity of extracting protons from water at the working electrode, the energy barrier for producing N₂ was decreased significantly compared with that of the ammonia (NH) route. This study provides further understanding of the distinct catalytic mechanisms for SAAs and nanoalloys in E-NRR and guides the design of high-performance catalysts for energy-related catalytic reactions.
explanation: The text is technically coherent with domain-specific jargon and precise numbers, which could come from either a human author or a sophisticated AI; given the detail and formal tone, the likelihood of AI-generation is moderate.
